items.title: Man killed as motorcycle bursts into flames in crash
items.summary: PUTATAN: A 47-year-old man was killed after his motorcycle caught fire in a crash involving a car along Jalan Putatan bound for Lokawi, early this morning. Putatan Fire and Rescue Station chief Musran Jabri said the department received an emergency call at 3.04am. He said five firefighters and a fire engine were deployed to the scene, about 5km from the station.

items.title: PM Anwar pledges China talks to rescue durian farmers hit by price slump in Johor
items.summary: TANGKAK, July 6 — The plight of durian farmers facing a drop in prices following a near-simultaneous peak durian season in Peninsular Malaysia has drawn the attention of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, who has pledged to raise the matter in discussions with Chinese Premier Li Qiang next month. He said the effort was aimed at expanding market access for Malaysian durian in China, thereby boosting demand and stabilising prices, which had currently affected growers’ income. Li Qiang, the Prime Minister of China, is a good friend.

items.title: AirBorneo files planned B737-800 flights to KL, Singapore from late July
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: AirBorneo Airways Sdn Bhd has filed planned Boeing B737-800 jet operations in the OAG airline schedules database with proposed services from Kuching to Kuala Lumpur and Singapore Changi from late July, according to aviation route updates platform AeroRoutes. AeroRoutes reported that the planned jet operation would see the Sarawak-owned airline commence Kuching-Kuala Lumpur services from July 20, and Kuching-Singapore Changi services from July 27, at the earliest. The planned Kuching-Kuala Lumpur schedule shows an afternoon rotation from July 20, with flight MY5005 departing Kuching at 1pm and arriving in Kuala Lumpur at 2.50pm.

items.title: 400,000 Year Four pupils to sit first Learning Matrix Assessment in October
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: About 400,000 Year Four pupils are expected to sit the inaugural Year Four Learning Matrix assessment over three days from Oct 6 to 8. The assessment will cover four core subjects — Bahasa Melayu, English, Mathematics and Science — as well as Chinese or Tamil for pupils in national-type schools. Ab Aziz said schools were responsible for registering all Year Four pupils to ensure sufficient question papers were prepared and no candidate was left out.

items.title: Firefighters rescue woman trapped under car as vehicle rolls forward while she opens gate in Ipoh home
items.summary: Perak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) assistant director of operations Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said they received an emergency call regarding the incident at 3.39pm before a team from the Pasir Puteh Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) rushed to the location. “Preliminary investigations found that the victim was believed to have been opening the gate to her house when the vehicle, with its engine running, moved suddenly, causing the victim to fall and get trapped under the vehicle,” he said in a statement yesterday. Sabarodzi said that the bystanders at the location tried to rescue the victim but were unsuccessful.

items.title: Pakistan delegation at Khamenei funeral draws effusive praise from Iran
items.summary: HYDERABAD: Pakistan's high-ranking delegation at the funeral of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei has won Iran's effusive praise, with the Iranian ambassador to Islamabad praying for "enduring friendship and brotherhood" between the two countries. Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif led the delegation, which included Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar, Chief of Army Staff Field Marshal Syed Asim Munir, Pakistan People's Party (PPP) chairman Bilawal Bhutto Zardari, National Assembly Speaker Sardar Ayaz Sadiq, Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi and Information Minister Attaullah Tarar. Khamenei was Iran's Supreme Leader from 1989 until his assassination at the age of 86.

items.title: Dengue cases in Malaysia rise nearly 30pc in H1 2026, MoH says outbreak remains under control
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — Dengue-related deaths in Malaysia have risen by 66.7 per cent this year as infections climbed nearly 30 per cent, although the Health Ministry (MoH) said the outbreak remains under control and follows the country’s usual transmission pattern. According to New Straits Times (NST), the ministry said 38,854 dengue cases were reported up to June 27, up 29.1 per cent from the same period last year. “The ministry does not base its public health interventions on fear, but rather on data-driven assessments and scientific evidence,” a ministry spokesman told NST when asked whether the situation could worsen.

items.title: In Dewan Rakyat today: Health insurance, EPF savings and volleyball talent hunt
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — Measures to strengthen health insurance regulations, the retirement savings of Employees Provident Fund (EPF) contributors and talent identification programmes for volleyball are among the key issues to be raised during today’s Dewan Rakyat sitting. Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun (PH-Port Dickson) will also ask the Finance Minister to explain the government’s strategy to ensure Malaysians have adequate retirement savings, particularly among EPF contributors, in view of the rising cost of living and the country’s ageing population by 2030. During the same session, Zakri Hassan (PN-Kangar) will ask the Youth and Sports Minister about the ministry’s efforts to identify and develop talent, particularly for indoor volleyball and beach volleyball.

items.title: Ukrainian capital Kyiv under missile attack, officials say
items.summary: KYIV: The Ukrainian capital Kyiv came under a Russian missile attack early on Monday and officials said residents were trapped inside a badly damaged building near the city centre. Mayor Vitali Klitschko, writing on Telegram, said a residential building had been hit in the historic Podil district. "People are trapped on the seventh to ninth floors," Klitschko said.

items.title: Ringgit opens firmer against greenback as traders reassess US rate hike prospects
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit opened firmer against the US dollar on Monday as traders reassessed the likelihood of a United States (US) interest rate hike following a weaker-than-expected US nonfarm payrolls (NFP) data. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the US Federal Reserve (Fed) chair's reluctance to provide forward guidance on interest rates also supported sentiment towards the ringgit. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: ROI-OPEC+ to boost crude output, but can it deliver and who will buy?
items.summary: LAUNCESTON, Australia: The decision by OPEC+ to increase crude oil production quotas for a fifth straight month from August raises two questions. But even looking at total shipments from the Middle East, it's clear that while volumes have lifted since the United States and Iran agreed to a 60-day ceasefire on June 17, they are still well below pre-war levels. June exports were 9.62 million bpd, roughly half of the 18.4 million bpd average for the three months leading up to the Iran conflict, according to data compiled by commodity analysts Kpler.

items.title: Teheran airspace to remain closed on monday for khamenei's funeral procession
items.summary: ISTANBUL: Iran will fully shut Teheran's airspace on Monday as part of funeral arrangements for slain Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Anadolu Ajansi citing ISNA news agency reported. Under the schedule announced by the Civil Aviation Organisation, regular flights at Teheran's Mehrabad International Airport and Imam Khomeini International Airport will be suspended on Monday as public ceremonies take place in the capital. On Tuesday, Mehrabad airport will resume normal operations, while Imam Khomeini airport will remain closed.

items.title: Health insurance rules, EPF retirement savings among Dewan Rakyat's focus today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Measures to strengthen health insurance regulations, boost retirement savings for Employees Provident Fund (EPF) contributors and enhance volleyball talent development are among the key issues set to be raised in the Dewan Rakyat today. According to the Order Paper on the Parliament portal, during Question Time, Tan Kok Wai (PH-Cheras) will ask the Finance Minister about measures to strengthen insurance regulations to protect policyholders, particularly those with critical illnesses and cancer, from policy cancellations and unfair claim rejections, while improving transparency and dispute resolution. Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun (PH-Port Dickson) will also ask the Finance Minister to outline the government's strategy to ensure Malaysians have adequate retirement savings, particularly EPF contributors, amid rising living costs and an ageing population.

items.title: Bluesify targets becoming Asean's most trusted cybersecurity provider
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Ekuinas-backed Bluesify Solutions Sdn Bhd is ramping up investments in artificial intelligence (AI), regional expansion and cybersecurity infrastructure as it targets becoming Southeast Asia's most trusted managed security service provider in the next three to five years. The cybersecurity firm is also establishing a second managed detection and response (MDR) centre in Cyberjaya, which is expected to commence operations in August to support rising demand for round-the-clock cybersecurity services across the region. Managing director Izman Haidi Mohd Ibrahim said the company aims to build a reputation for anticipating and neutralising increasingly sophisticated cyber threats while strengthening its presence across Asean.

items.title: SME credit risk rises as cost pressures intensify
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Credit risk among Malaysia's small and medium enterprises (SMEs) is rising as businesses grapple with persistent cost pressures, raising concerns over cash flows and debt-servicing capacity. SME Association of Malaysia national president Dr Chin Chee Seong said the increase reflects mounting financial strain rather than irresponsible borrowing behaviour. A recent survey by the association showed that 83.3 per cent of SMEs reported operating cost increases of more than 10 per cent, while 63.7 per cent experienced increases exceeding 30 per cent.

items.title: Sex offender list should be 'tiered model'
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia should review access to its sex offender registry and introduce a tiered, risk-based disclosure system to better protect children and other vulnerable groups, said criminologist Datuk Dr P. Sundramoorthy. He said high-risk, repeat or predatory offenders who continue to pose a threat could be subject to wider community notification, while information on lower-risk offenders should remain restricted. Sundramoorthy said greater access could enhance public safety by helping parents, educational institutions and community organisations protect children and adolescents from individuals assessed as posing a continuing risk.

items.title: Air Astana mulls KL return
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Air Astana remains interested in returning to Kuala Lumpur as the Kazakhstan's national carrier continues evaluating new destinations and expanding its international network. Air Astana Group chief executive officer (CEO) Ibrahim Canliel said Kuala Lumpur remains a destination that Air Astana would like to serve again although other opportunities currently rank higher within its network development plans. Air Astana first launched direct services between Almaty and Kuala Lumpur in October 2009.

items.title: Why the world's chokepoints still govern the global economy
items.summary: For almost three decades, globalisation encouraged governments and businesses alike to believe that technology, markets and just-in-time supply chains had conquered geography. Production shifted to wherever costs were lowest, capital flowed with unprecedented ease, and the physical map appeared increasingly subordinate to the digital economy. Meanwhile, the Strait of Malacca remains one of the world's busiest maritime corridors and the principal maritime gateway linking East Asia with the Indian Ocean, the Middle East and Europe.

items.title: Venezuela quake deaths pass 3,000, attention turns to mourning, burials
items.summary: CATIA LA MAR, Venezuela: Deaths from Venezuela's devastating twin earthquakes rose to at least 3,342 on Sunday as officials began to bury dozens of bodies left unidentified 11 days after the disaster. The June 24 double shocks, one of Latin America's worst quake disasters, toppled scores of buildings in the La Guaira coastal area north of Caracas and left thousands of people missing. In a secluded area of La Esperanza cemetery in La Guaira, gravediggers buried more than 150 bodies still unidentified since the earthquakes, AFP journalists saw.

items.title: Reports of 'major damages' from super typhoon on US island Rota
items.summary: SAIPAN: Authorities on Rota said Monday they had received reports from residents of "major damages" as Super Typhoon Bavi roared over the US Pacific island. We are experiencing heavy winds and flooding here at Rota's MOC. Some people already reporting major damages," the Rota mayor's operations center public information officer Lou Rosario said.--AFP © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Dengue survivors turn painful lessons into action
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: What three dengue patients thought was a normal fever quickly became a painful ordeal, leaving them bedridden, exhausted and, in one case, with liver complications. TV3 cameraman Mohammed Fairuz Yazeer, 35, sought treatment at a Kota Kinabalu private hospital about a week of fever, chills, diarrhoea, loss of appetite, severe joint pain and rashes. "I really thought it was just a normal fever.

items.title: UK tightens rules on overseas political donations to stop 'dodgy funding'
items.summary: LONDON: Britain tightened its rules on overseas political donations on Monday, as part of efforts to stop foreign money influencing elections, and as concerns swirl around what Housing Minister Steve Reed called "dodgy funding." Last year, the government ordered a review into foreign financial interference in politics after a former politician in Reform UK was jailed for taking bribes to make pro-Russia speeches and statements. Farage's Reform UK, which has topped national opinion polls for over a year, has said no rules were broken over the donation to Farage from crypto investor Christopher Harborne, who provided about two-thirds of Reform's funding last year, according to Electoral Commission data.

items.title: When dengue turns deadly: Doctors warn against lowering your guard [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Blood soaked through a hospital bed as doctors rushed to save a dengue patient whose condition deteriorated within hours. She said not every dengue patient was as fortunate. "Dengue is unpredictable.

items.title: Malaysia records sharp rise in dengue cases and deaths
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Dengue cases have increased significantly this year, while deaths have risen sharply. Health authorities, however, said the outbreak remains under control. The Health Ministry said deaths increased from 18 to 30, which it attributed to the dominant dengue virus serotype shifting from DENV-2 to DENV-3, alongside urbanisation, climate factors, persistent mosquito breeding sites and increased population movement.

items.title: Johor polls: BN, PH promising 'stability'
items.summary: For BN, stability means continuity under caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, backed by the state's strong economic performance and development plans. PH, meanwhile, is pitching a different proposition — that Johor stands to gain more with a state government aligned with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's administration in Putrajaya, promising closer federal-state cooperation that could accelerate development in Johor. PH election director Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said while Johor has recorded the highest gross domestic product growth in the country, this has not fully translated into high-paying jobs.

items.title: German legacy in Penang stretches back 200 years, from forgotten settlers to modern industry
items.summary: The Germans first arrived in Penang more than 200 years ago, sometime in 1801, according to Malaysian German Society committee member Clement Liang. “During the First World War, Germany became Britain’s enemy, so the whole German community was chased out,” Liang told Malay Mail . The first German Association established in 1898 before the Germans left during World War 1.

items.title: German footprints in Penang reveal hidden colonial-era stories along George Town heritage trail
items.summary: In the early 1800s, German traders, missionaries, engineers, photographers and writers arrived on the island. This lesser-known chapter of Penang’s history is now highlighted through the German Heritage Trail, which maps out sites linked to the community, said Malaysian German Society committee member Clement Liang. A map of the German Heritage Trail in Penang courtesy of a booklet prepared by the Penang Heritage Trust.

items.title: Kg Istana all for digital push to boost ecotourism
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: Residents of Kampung Istana in Penggaram here welcome government efforts to develop the village's ecotourism potential through digital marketing training and improved Internet connectivity. Village Development and Security Committee chairman Abd Jalil Ali said the Communications Ministry's initiative would greatly benefit the village's 1,300 residents. Earlier, Fahmi said Kampung Istana had the potential to become an ecotourism destination, supported by digital marketing training for entrepreneurs and better Internet access.

items.title: Dengue survivors turn painful lessons into action
items.summary: As dengue cases continue to rise, residents in parts of the Klang Valley are stepping up efforts to eliminate mosquito breeding sites, fearing a disease that can strike without warning and leave families devastated. A few streets away, student Siti Maryam, 26, said dengue prevention had become part of her family's routine through regular clean-ups and measures to reduce mosquito exposure. Although she considers the neighbourhood relatively well maintained, preventing dengue requires collective effort.

items.title: Merdeka 118 EV hub opens
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Yinson GreenTech has launched a 32-bay alternating current (AC) electric vehicle (EV) charging hub at Menara Merdeka 118. Operated by chargEV — Yinson GreenTech's electric mobility arm — the facility is Malaysia's largest single-site AC charging hub, featuring 22-kilowatt (kW) chargers. Yinson GreenTech chief executive officer Louisa Brady said the hub aims to expand EV charging access at key urban locations.

items.title: N. Korea's Kim oversees weapons test of repaired warship
items.summary: SEOUL: North Korean leader Kim Jong-un oversaw weapons tests of the 5,000-tonne destroyer Kang Kon, including cruise missile launches and "electronic warfare means", state media reported yesterday. The tests took place on Friday, the state-run Korean Central News Agency (KCNA) said, less than two weeks after another 5,000-tonne destroyer, the Choe Hyon, entered military service. During that commissioning ceremony, Kim vowed to equip his navy with nuclear weapons and develop new 10,000-tonne warships.

items.title: Venezuela quake survivor 'reborn' after eight days in rubble
items.summary: CARACAS: Hernan Gil was working his shift as a security guard in the basement of a building in Venezuela on the afternoon of June 24 when he felt the earth shake. Gil felt everything crumble and stones hit the back of his head and eye. Gil couldn't see anything or hear anyone.

items.title: Infernos devastate forests as Europe's temperatures rise again
items.summary: BARCELONA: Wildfires raged across southern Europe on Sunday, forcing thousands of people to evacuate their homes and prompting Tour de France officials to ban spectators from a stage of the race. Some 10,500 people were being evacuated from their homes near the city of Perpignan in southwestern France as firefighters tried to control a blaze that has devoured 1,650 hectares, authorities said. "We started seeing smoke around 10:30 pm, then it kept coming closer and closer.

items.title: White supremacist march in DC just 'messy' democracy, official says
items.summary: WASHINGTON: A top Trump administration official on Sunday shrugged off a July Fourth parade by masked white supremacists in Washington as an example of "messy" democracy that fell under the banner of constitutionally protected free speech. In striking scenes as Americans celebrated the nation's 250th birthday on Saturday, hundreds of masked men carrying Confederate battle flags or US flags and sporting logos of the white supremacist Patriot Front movement were seen marching through parts of Washington, shouting "Reclaim America!" Washington's Metropolitan Police Department said the white nationalist group marched briefly through neighborhoods around the US Capitol and left the city before 11:00 am.

items.title: More than 100 detained as leftist groups hold anti-Nato protests in Turkiye
items.summary: REUTERS ANKARA: Turkish authorities detained more than 100 people taking part in an anti-Nato protest march organised by the Communist Party of Turkiye (TKP) on Sunday, ahead of an alliance summit in Ankara next week, the party said in a statement. Turkiye will host leaders from the 32 allied countries, as well as officials from Nato's partners, in Ankara for a summit on Tuesday and Wednesday. In a statement, the TKP said it had organised the protest march in Ankara's central Kizilay square, adding that more than 100 party members, including administrators, had been detained.

items.title: Jewellery worth millions stolen in French museum burglary
items.summary: "Around twenty pieces of jewellery were stolen. The loss is currently being assessed but could amount to several million euros, likely close to four million," the source said. Another source close to the investigation said the stolen jewellery was crystal, without precious gems, and could not be melted down.

items.title: Show must go on: RTM-led Johor dialogue set to go ahead this Tuesday night sans Onn Hafiz
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 5 — The Johor State Election Dialogue will proceed as scheduled on Tuesday (July 7) despite Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi being understood to be unable to attend due to other commitments. The programme, jointly organised by RTM, Astro Awani and Sinar Harian , is scheduled to take place at the RTM Auditorium, Johor Broadcasting Department, from 8 pm. Earlier, RTM , Astro Awani and Sinar Harian had extended official invitations to Onn Hafiz and Maszlee to participate in the Johor state election dialogue.

items.title: Contacted by an ‘MACC officer’? Verify first if genuine or fake, commission advises public
items.summary: “The public is reminded to be cautious if they encounter individuals claiming to be MACC officers,” according to the statement. The MACC urges the public to verify the identity of MACC officers through official channels before providing any information or cooperation. The MACC emphasises that impersonating an MACC officer is a criminal offence.

items.title: Health Ministry rolls out RM1.45m sea ambulance and birthing unit for Pulau Tuba islanders
items.summary: LANGKAWI, July 5 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) has rolled out a RM1.45 million sea ambulance and an Emergency Birthing Unit (EBU) on Pulau Tuba, boosting emergency care access for over 5,000 island residents. In a statement today, MOH said the 48-foot sea ambulance, which began operations on May 20, is custom-built for patient transport and equipped with modern emergency care facilities. The sea ambulance now enables faster, safer and more efficient patient transfers, strengthening emergency response for the entire island community, it added.

items.title: Pakatan candidate Felicia Poh puts youth and entrepreneurship at the heart of Penggaram campaign
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, July 5 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for Penggaram, Felicia Poh Rui Ling, is making the younger generation the focus of her four priorities to empower the local community should she emerge victorious in the upcoming 16th Johor state election. Felicia, 28, said her first mission would be to shift young people’s mindset from merely seeking employment to venturing into entrepreneurship, particularly through micro, small and medium enterprises. She also expressed her commitment to provide comprehensive exposure to government initiatives, including access to financing and entrepreneurship development programmes, to build young people’s confidence in pursuing business opportunities.

items.title: Short RM100,000, top MRSM student who nearly turns down Al-Azhar offer to study medicine, gets MARA lifeline
items.summary: MARA chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said the agency would explore the best option to enable Mohamad Solihin to pursue medical studies, either at Al-Azhar University or at Universiti Sains Malaysia Health Campus (USMKK) under MARA sponsorship. “The second option is to pursue medical studies at USMKK under MARA sponsorship,” he said during a video call with Mohamad Solihin at his home in Kampung Kubang Keranji here today. “We indeed focus on students like Mohamad Solihin.

items.title: Conned in Teluk Intan, nabbed in Semenyih: Family of three dupe 67-year-old of RM8,000 in jewellery at hospital with fake aid offer
items.summary: IPOH, July 5 — Police arrested a woman and her two children for allegedly cheating a senior citizen of jewellery worth RM8,000 by offering a bogus welfare aid package at a hospital in Teluk Intan on June 29. Perak police chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said the suspects, aged 22 to 50, were detained during a raid at an apartment in Semenyih, Selangor, on July 3. The suspect offered RM2,000 monthly community welfare assistance.

items.title: Anwar vows to settle Felda second-generation housing woes before term ends
items.summary: SEGAMAT, July 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has pledged to resolve the long-standing issue of providing housing sites for the second generation of Felda settlers. He said its implementation, however, requires the cooperation of state governments, as land matters and the provision of basic infrastructure fall under their jurisdiction. Also present were Selangor Menteri Besar and PKR vice-president Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek, and Deputy National Unity Minister and Segamat MP R. Yuneswaran.

items.title: LRT3 is running, but Shah Alam Stadium–Aeon Mall walkway? Complete tomorrow, says Selangor exco
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 5 — The pedestrian walkway to connect LRT3 station Stadium Shah Alam to Aeon Mall along Persiaran Sukan is expected to be fully completed tomorrow, a Selangor government official said today. The Shah Alam City Council (MBSA) is also expected to continue working on this pedestrian pathway along another road, Selangor Youth, Sports and Entrepreneurship executive councillor Mohd Najwan Halimi added. “For your information, the construction of the pedestrian walkway that connects the LRT station Stadium Shah Alam to Aeon Mall along Persiaran Sukan, Section 13 is now being carried out and is expected to be fully completed tomorrow.

items.title: Minister says Jakim to update Muslim funeral guidelines for all states after burial delayed in Selangor
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, July 5 — The Department of Islamic Development Malaysia (Jakim) is refining funeral management guidelines to serve as a nationwide reference, following an issue that went viral recently. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan said the effort takes into account current needs as well as state enactments and laws. On the viral issue involving funeral management in Selangor, he said the matter has been left to the state religious authorities – the Selangor Islamic Religious Department (Jais) and the Selangor Islamic Religious Council (Mais) – to handle.

items.title: Labourer in Kelantan gets four years’ jail, RM6,000 fine for abducting man over brother’s debt and stealing Volkswagen Jetta
items.summary: He was charged under Section 365 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 34 of the same Code, which carries a penalty of up to seven years’ imprisonment and a fine. The court sentenced the accused to four years’ jail from the date of his arrest on June 16, along with a RM3,000 fine for the kidnapping offence, with an additional three months’ jail if the fine is not paid. However, the court sentenced him to three years’ jail and a RM3,000 fine, with an additional three months’ imprisonment if the fine is unpaid.

items.title: Move over Musang King because Mardi is prepping three top-secret premium durian hybrids
items.summary: KUALA KANGSAR, July 5 — The Malaysian Agricultural Research and Development Institute (Mardi) has planted about 25,000 trees of the MRDI Super 88 (MS88) hybrid durian nationwide to date. “The MS88 is one of three premium durian varieties developed by Mardi since the early 2000s, together with MDUR78 and MDUR79, which remain popular among durian enthusiasts,” he told reporters after the Duriotourism Mardi Kuala Kangsar programme with the media at the Mardi Agrotechnology Park here today. In another development, Mohamad Zabawi said Mardi is selecting three new hybrid durian varieties with potential to be introduced as premium types to meet industry demand and consumer preference.

items.title: Mohamad Hasan hints BN could shake up ‘traditional seats’ for Negeri Sembilan polls
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 5 — Barisan Nasional (BN) is likely to make changes by no longer retaining “traditional seats” in the division of constituencies contested by the coalition in the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election. BN deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said the move was due to the composition of voters in most constituencies having changed significantly. That is my view as the Negeri Sembilan BN chairman: do a seat swap because we must give voters an opportunity.

items.title: Fahmi: Johor among priorities as MCMC moves to improve internet coverage with shared networks
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, July 5 — The Communications Ministry, through the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), plans to expand the implementation of the Multi-Operator Core Network (MOCN) nationwide to improve connectivity in areas with limited internet coverage. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the initiative would enable telecommunications companies to collaborate, particularly in extending coverage to rural, remote and underserved areas with weak signal reception. “In Johor, we are currently identifying several areas for implementation.

items.title: Khaled Nordin says Hobart Camp blast investigation report will be submitted by mid‑month
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, July 5 — The investigation report into the grenade explosion that claimed the lives of two military personnel during a training exercise at Hobart Camp in Gurun last month is expected to be submitted to the Ministry of Defence by the middle of this month. In the incident at about 10.57am on June 16, the two personnel involved — Corporal Norazmi Abu Bakar of the Sixth Battalion, Royal Malay Regiment (RAMD), and Private Siti Khadijah Sungip of the First Squadron, Royal Engineers Regiment (RAJD) — sustained severe injuries in the explosion during the training exercise. Two weeks later, on June 29, another incident occurred at the same camp when a firearm discharged during a combat enhancement training (LPT) exercise, injuring two military personnel.

items.title: Defence Ministry wary of delay tactics as naval supplier seeks talks over cancelled RM1.06b deal, says minister
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, July 5 — The Defence Ministry has stated that the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) supplier has requested discussions over the RM1.06 billion claim stemming from the cancelled procurement for the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN). However, Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the ministry is cautious about any discussion being used as an excuse or tactic to delay the claims process. Following the cancellation, Malaysia is claiming RM1.06 billion from KDA, comprising EUR129.86 million (RM604 million) in direct claims for payments already made, plus EUR96.26 million (RM448.12 million) in indirect claims covering cost implications and knock-on effects from the failed supply.

items.title: From shopping carts to prayer mats: Minister wants Friday prayers expanded to malls nationwide
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, July 5 — Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan has welcomed states across the country to hold Friday prayers at shopping malls, following the positive response to the initiative at several locations in the Federal Territories. Zulkifli said the initiative was first introduced in the Federal Territories through a collaboration between the Federal Territories Islamic Religious Department (Jawi) and the Federal Territories Islamic Religious Council (MAIWP), as it falls under the jurisdiction of the two agencies. “So far, Friday prayers have been held at several shopping malls, including IOI City Mall, Alamanda Shopping Centre and Suria KLCC, while discussions are underway to expand the initiative to several other locations.

items.title: Police: Shah Alam college failed to check employee’s criminal record before hiring him in teen molest, sodomy case
items.summary: “As a result, the college was unaware that the individual had a record involving sexual offences,” district police chief Assistant Commissioner Sarudin Samah said. Sarudin said police will record statements from the college’s management to assist the investigation. Sarudin said the suspect is currently remanded until July 11 and that police are awaiting medical reports.

items.title: Johor polls: Bukit Batu Pakatan candidate steps up campaign to lift voter turnout above 60pc
items.summary: KULAI, July 5 — With the Johor state election polling day just a few days away, incumbent Bukit Batu state assemblyman from Pakatan Harapan (PH), Arthur Chiong Sen Sern, is intensifying his campaign to ensure that the voter turnout on July 11 exceeds 60 per cent. “I and the (Bukit Batu PH) machinery are all out campaigning every day. N51 Bukit Batu: 49,963 voters 1.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia and Singapore to launch digital immigration system and new lanes by January
items.summary: MUAR, July 5 — Malaysia and Singapore are targeting the launch of a new digital immigration system and additional lanes by January next year to address congestion at border checkpoints more comprehensively. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the initiative will be jointly launched with his Singapore counterpart Lawrence Wong once all technical preparations and infrastructure are completed. “The Immigration Department will facilitate the movement, and once the system is ready, it will include new travel arrangements from Singapore to Johor Bahru,” he said.

items.title: Anwar: DAP never disputes Malay rights and supports a government for all races
items.summary: MUAR, July 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim stressed that DAP has never disputed the rights of the Malays, thus refuting the narrative of certain quarters who are trying to portray the party as a racist organisation. Anwar, who is also the chairman of Pakatan Harapan (PH), said as proof, DAP has never objected to its intention to gazette 20.23 hectares of land in Bandar Malaysia as Malay Reserve Land. Poor Chinese should be helped, poor Indians should be helped, the majority of poor Malays should be helped,” he said at the Meeting Anwar Programme at a restaurant hall here today.

items.title: Thunderstorms and heavy rain to hit KL and several states until 4pm, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 5 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds expected to hit Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya and eight states until 4pm today. MetMalaysia, in a statement, said the warning covers Kelantan (Jeli, Kuala Krai and Gua Musang), Terengganu (Besut, Hulu Terengganu, Dungun and Kemaman), and Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Raub, Bentong, Pekan and Rompin). The warning also applies to Selangor, involving the districts of Hulu Selangor, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat, Hulu Langat and Sepang.

items.title: MOHE: International students form 12.6pc of IPT enrolment and do not reduce opportunities for Malaysians
items.summary: Data from the MyMoHES system shows total enrolment at 1,264,541 students, comprising 159,138 international students (12.6 per cent) and 1,105,403 Malaysian students (87.4 per cent). At postgraduate level, enrolment totals 114,601 students, comprising 75,929 Malaysians (66.26 per cent) and 38,672 international students (33.74 per cent). Of this, 145,727 (78 per cent) are local students and 41,097 (22 per cent) are international students.

items.title: Sultan Sallehuddin marks 84th birthday by conferring top honours on military and police chiefs
items.summary: Meanwhile, Kedah legislative assembly speaker Zubir Ahmad was conferred the Darjah Dato’ Setia Sultan Sallehuddin Kedah (D.S.S.S), which carries the title Dato’. Four individuals received the Darjah Dato’ Setia Diraja Kedah (D.S.D.K), which carries the title Dato’. Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah was conferred the Darjah Dato’ Setia Pahlawan (D.D.S.P), which also carries the title Dato’.

items.title: Strong westerly winds expected to hit Sabah, Sarawak and the peninsula from Wednesday, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 5 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) is forecasting a concentration of strong westerly winds that could potentially bring bad weather in several areas starting this Wednesday. The bad weather is also expected to hit the northern and eastern areas of Peninsular Malaysia on July 10 to 12, 2026. According to MetMalaysia, the situation could result in strong winds and rough seas in the South China Sea, Phuket waters and north of the Strait of Malacca.
